name: Kai Tam (Simple Chicken Soup)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup phak thong (pumpkin), cut into chunks
1 tbsp prik ki nu daeng haeng (crumbled dried red chilis)
recipeInstructions:
1. This is a chicken soup my wife calls "mother's all-purpose sickness cure." Mothers worldwide seem to agree that sending a sick child to bed with this soup cures most ailments, at least the common ones like being sick of school.
2. Prepare the chicken by washing it carefully and cutting off the drumsticks and wings. Set aside the bones for making more stock later.
3. Bring the stock to a boil. Add all ingredients except the chicken (sapparot, phak thong, shallots, lime juice, tamarind juice, dried chilis, garlic) and simmer, covered, for about 10 minutes.
4. Add the chicken breasts and legs. Return the mixture to a boil, then remove from heat and let stand, covered, for an additional 30 minutes.
5. Serve hot with white rice and a selection of dipping sauces garnished with coriander leaves.
